title: Distinct Values Count with df['col'].nunique()
naive_code:
# Slow manual set conversion for distinct count
unique_count = len(set(df['email'].dropna()))
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Native Distinct Counter
unique_count = df['email'].nunique()
hinglish_explanation: List set conversion me null values aur memory overhead ka issue hota hai. `df['col'].nunique()` instant C-speed par distinct non-null values count karta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain pandas nunique() vs unique() and how to count distinct values across multiple columns simultaneously.

title: SQL-style Filtering with df.query()
naive_code:
# Messy bracketed indexing with df repeated everywhere
filtered = df[(df['age'] > 18) & (df['city'] == 'Mumbai')]
wizard_code:
# Clean SQL-Style Query String
filtered = df.query('age > 18 and city == "Mumbai"')
hinglish_explanation: Multiple conditions me double-bracketed syntax padhna mushkil hota hai. `df.query()` code ko readable banati hai aur backend numexpr ki wajah se fast execute karti hai!
ai_prompt: Refactor complex Pandas boolean indexing code to use df.query() with dynamic parameter variables using @variable syntax.

title: Multi-Column Sorting with Different Directions
naive_code:
# Sorting twice sequentially (unstable & slow)
df_sorted = df.sort_values(by='salary', ascending=False).sort_values(by='dept')
wizard_code:
# 1-Pass Multi-Column Directional Sort
df_sorted = df.sort_values(by=['dept', 'salary'], ascending=[True, False])
hinglish_explanation: Baar baar sort karne ki jagah `ascending=[True, False]` list pass karo. Pehle department A-Z sort hoga aur same dept me highest salary sabse upar dikhegi!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate Pandas sort_values multi-column sorting with nulls placement using na_position='first' or 'last'.

title: Clean String Method Chaining with .str.strip().str.lower()
naive_code:
# Loop over rows to clean whitespace and lowercase
cleaned = [name.strip().lower() for name in df['name']]
df['name'] = cleaned
wizard_code:
# Vectorized String Method Chain
df['name'] = df['name'].str.strip().str.lower()
hinglish_explanation: Python loops string cleaning me performance ruin karte hain. `.str` accessor vectorwise saare rows ke spaces trim karke lowercase 1 line me kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Show vector string operations in Pandas using .str accessor for regex replacement and case normalization.

title: Safe Immutable Column Creation with df.assign()
naive_code:
# Mutates original dataframe directly (SettingWithCopyWarning risk)
df['total'] = df['price'] * df['qty']
wizard_code:
# Safe Chainable Column Creation
new_df = df.assign(total=df['price'] * df['qty'])
hinglish_explanation: Direct assignment se baseline DataFrame alter ho jata hai. `df.assign()` naya reference return karta hai, jo functional method pipelines me original data ko safe rakhta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain pandas df.assign() method for non-destructive column creation and pipeline chaining.

title: Safe Regex Pattern Matching with na=False
naive_code:
# Throws ValueError or crashes when column contains NaN values
good_reviews = df[df['rev'].str.contains('good')]
wizard_code:
# Safe NaN-Ignored Pattern Search
good_reviews = df[df['rev'].str.contains('good', na=False)]
hinglish_explanation: Jab string column me missing/NaN values hon, normal `.contains()` crash kar jata hai. `na=False` pass karne se NaN values non-match (False) treat hoti hain bina query phate!
ai_prompt: Show how to use pandas str.contains with regular expressions and case insensitivity (case=False).

title: Smart Specific Column Deduplication with drop_duplicates()
naive_code:
# Manual loop or grouping to keep first email entry
df_unique = df.groupby('email').first().reset_index()
wizard_code:
# Native Targeted Deduplication
df_unique = df.drop_duplicates(subset=['email'], keep='first')
hinglish_explanation: Pura row match karne ki jagah `subset=['email']` specify karo. Sirf unique emails retain hongi aur first record survive karega baaki drop ho jayenge!
ai_prompt: Compare pandas drop_duplicates keep='first' vs keep='last' vs keep=False.

title: Forward Fill Missing Values with ffill
wizard_code:
# Instant Vectorized Forward Fill
df['col'] = df['col'].ffill()
hinglish_explanation: Time-series ya financial tables me missing gaps ko pichhli valid row ki value se bharne ke liye `ffill()` instant vectorized method hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate pandas ffill() vs bfill() for missing data imputation in time-series.

title: Pair Lists into Dictionary Instantly with dict(zip())
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Zip Dictionary Pairing
mapping = dict(zip(names, ages))
hinglish_explanation: Do parallel lists ko key-value lookup dict banane ke liye loop mat chalao. `dict(zip(keys, values))` 1 line me mapping dictionary taiyar karta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain zip() pairing in Python and how it simplifies map building for pandas series replace.

title: Get Most Frequent Value (Mode) with .mode()[0]
naive_code:
# Counting frequencies to find max item manually
most_common = df['col'].value_counts().index[0]
wizard_code:
# Direct Statistical Mode
most_frequent = df['col'].mode()[0]
hinglish_explanation: Category ya categorical column me sabse zyada repeat hone wali value (Mode) directly `.mode()[0]` se bina manual counting index math ke mil jaati hai!
ai_prompt: Show how to use pandas mode() to fill missing categorical values in a dataframe.

title: Percentage Frequency Counts with normalize=True
naive_code:
# Manual calculation of counts divided by total rows
counts = df['city'].value_counts()
percentages = (counts / len(df)) * 100
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Count + Percentage Generator
percentages = df['city'].value_counts(normalize=True) * 100
hinglish_explanation: Raw count ki jagah relative share (%) nikalne ke liye `normalize=True` pass karo. Counts calculate hone ke sath automatic % distribution mil jata hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate pandas value_counts(normalize=True, dropna=False) for total percentage distribution.

title: Selective Column Renaming with rename()
naive_code:
# Reassigning full column list (error-prone if list order changes)
df.columns = ['id', 'new_name', 'age', 'salary_amount']
wizard_code:
# Safe Key-Value Mapper Renaming
df.rename(columns={'old_col': 'new_col', 'sal': 'salary'}, inplace=True)
hinglish_explanation: Saare column names rewrite karne se schema order break ho sakta hai. `rename(columns={'old':'new'})` dict pass karne se baki columns untouched rehte hain!
ai_prompt: Show how to rename pandas columns using functions like df.rename(columns=str.lower).

title: Un-pivot Wide Tables to Long Format with df.melt()
wizard_code:
# Fast Vectorized Table Reshaping
tidy_df = df.melt(id_vars=['Prod'], var_name='Month', value_name='Sales')
hinglish_explanation: Wide spreadsheet format (multiple month columns) ko clean long tidy format me convert karne ke liye `df.melt()` best tabular reshaping tool hai!
ai_prompt: Explain pandas melt() vs pivot() with a practical example of tidying wide dataset tables.

title: Excel-style Dynamic Pivoting with pd.pivot_table()
naive_code:
# Complex groupby and unstack combo
pivoted = df.groupby(['dept', 'gender'])['salary'].mean().unstack()
wizard_code:
# Clean Excel-Style Pivot Engine
pivoted = pd.pivot_table(df, values='salary', index='dept', columns='gender', aggfunc='mean', fill_value=0)
hinglish_explanation: `pd.pivot_table()` bina multiple `.unstack()` chains ke instant 2D cross-tabulated aggregate matrix bina overhead ke bana deta hai!
ai_prompt: Create an Excel-style multi-metric pivot table in Pandas using aggfunc={'salary': 'mean', 'bonus': 'sum'}.

title: Row-wise Conditional Classification with apply()
naive_code:
# Slow row loop appending to list
status = []
for age in df['age']:
    status.append('Senior' if age > 60 else 'Junior')
df['status'] = status
wizard_code:
# Vectorized Row-wise Lambda Classification
df['status'] = df['age'].apply(lambda x: 'Senior' if x > 60 else 'Junior')
hinglish_explanation: Custom row logic apply karne ke liye manual list append se bacho. `.apply(lambda)` series level par elementwise transformation fast handle karti hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate pandas apply() vs np.select() for row-wise conditional logic performance.

title: Inclusive Numeric Filtering with df['col'].between()
naive_code:
# Long multi-operator range condition
filtered = df[(df['salary'] >= 30000) & (df['salary'] <= 50000)]
wizard_code:
# Clean Inclusive Range Selector
filtered = df[df['salary'].between(30000, 50000)]
hinglish_explanation: >= aur <= double conditions attach karne ki jagah `between(min, max)` use karo. Code clean aur readable rehne ke saath inclusive range auto-select karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show pandas series between() method with inclusive='left' or inclusive='neither' options.

title: Multi-Value Matching with df['col'].isin()
naive_code:
# Multiple OR conditions chained together
filtered = df[(df['city'] == 'Mumbai') | (df['city'] == 'Delhi') | (df['city'] == 'Bangalore')]
wizard_code:
# Vectorized List-Based Match
target_cities = ['Mumbai', 'Delhi', 'Bangalore']
filtered = df[df['city'].isin(target_cities)]
hinglish_explanation: Multiple `OR (|)` conditions lagana unmaintainable hota hai. Targets ko list me dalkar `isin(list)` pass karne se instant match ho jata hai!
ai_prompt: Explain pandas isin() for filtering dataframes using dictionary column-wise criteria.

title: Top N Rows without Full Sorting via nlargest()
naive_code:
# Heavy full table sort just to slice top 3 rows
top_3 = df.sort_values(by='score', ascending=False).head(3)
wizard_code:
# Heap-based O(N log K) Top N Extraction
top_3 = df.nlargest(3, 'score')
hinglish_explanation: Pura DataFrame `sort_values` karne me O(N log N) overhead lagta hai. `df.nlargest(k, col)` min-heap algorithm se top K items instant slice kar leta hai!
ai_prompt: Benchmark pandas nlargest() vs sort_values().head() on a 1-million row dataframe.

title: Fast Random Sampling with df.sample()
naive_code:
# Importing random module and indexing numpy choices manually
import random
indices = random.sample(range(len(df)), 5)
sample_df = df.iloc[indices]
wizard_code:
# Native Random Row Sampler
sample_df = df.sample(n=5)  # Or df.sample(frac=0.1) for 10% sample
hinglish_explanation: Dataset se quick test sample ya 10% subset lene ke liye manual random indexing ki jagah `df.sample(n=5)` ya `df.sample(frac=0.1)` ka istemal karo!
ai_prompt: Show how to get reproducible random samples in pandas using random_state seed parameter.

title: Continuous Binning into Categories with pd.cut()
wizard_code:
# Vectorized Bin Segmentation
df['age_group'] = pd.cut(df['age'], bins=[0, 18, 100], labels=['Minor', 'Adult'])
hinglish_explanation: Numbers (e.g. Age, Income) ko ranges me divide karke category label dene ke liye `pd.cut()` discrete binning engine best hai!
ai_prompt: Compare pandas pd.cut() vs pd.qcut() for quantile-based equal-frequency binning.

title: Robust Date Parsing with errors='coerce'
naive_code:
# Standard conversion crashes on invalid date strings
df['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['date_string'])
wizard_code:
# Safe Date Conversion with Garbage to NaT
df['date'] = pd.to_datetime(df['date_string'], errors='coerce')
hinglish_explanation: Date column me garbage text ya wrong formats aane par code phat jata hai. `errors='coerce'` invalid dates ko safely `NaT` (Not a Time) me convert kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Show pandas to_datetime with custom format strings and handling of mixed date formats.

title: Index Swapping with set_index() & reset_index()
naive_code:
# Manual column re-creation and index dropping
df['id'] = df.index
df.index = range(len(df))
wizard_code:
# Clean Index-Column Interchange
df = df.set_index('user_id').reset_index()
hinglish_explanation: Index aur Normal Column ke beech variables move karne ke liye `set_index('col')` aur `reset_index()` standard Pythonic utilities hain!
ai_prompt: Explain pandas reset_index(drop=True) to avoid creating duplicate index columns.

title: VLOOKUP Style Merging with df.merge()
naive_code:
# Slow dictionary map lookup across dataframes
user_dict = dict(zip(users['id'], users['email']))
orders['email'] = orders['user_id'].map(user_dict)
wizard_code:
# Fast Relational Left Join
merged_df = orders.merge(users[['id', 'email']], on='id', how='left')
hinglish_explanation: Excel VLOOKUP ka exact Python equivalent `df.merge(how='left')` hai. Yeh C-optimized relational join algorithm runs karta hai!
ai_prompt: Compare pandas merge() vs join() vs concat() for combining relational datasets.

title: Extract Digits from String Columns with Regex str.extract()
naive_code:
# Custom string parsing loop for extracting numbers
nums = [''.join(filter(str.isdigit, text)) for text in df['text']]
df['amount'] = nums
wizard_code:
# Vectorized Regex Extraction
df['amount'] = df['text'].str.extract(r'(\d+)').astype(float)
hinglish_explanation: Dirty text strings (e.g. 'Price $500 USD') se numbers pull karne ke liye `.str.extract(r'(\d+)')` 1-liner regex extraction master stroke hai!
ai_prompt: Show examples of pandas str.extractall() vs str.extract() for capturing regex groups.

title: Explode List-Columns into Rows with df.explode()
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner List Unnesting to Rows
expanded_df = df.explode('tags')
hinglish_explanation: Jab kisi cell me lists stored ho (`['python', 'sql']`), `df.explode('col')` har element ke liye alag individual row create kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate pandas explode() for unnesting JSON payload list columns.

title: Period Difference Calculations with df['col'].diff()
naive_code:
# Subtracting shifted series manually
delta = df['revenue'] - df['revenue'].shift(1)
wizard_code:
# Direct 1-Liner Row Difference
delta = df['revenue'].diff()
hinglish_explanation: Today vs Yesterday revenue diff (delta) nikalne ke liye `diff()` direct short utility hai jo auto 1-step lag difference calculate karti hai!
ai_prompt: Show pandas diff(periods=7) for week-over-week difference calculation.

title: Instant Percentage Growth Rate with pct_change()
naive_code:
# Manual formula calculation (Today - Prev) / Prev * 100
growth = ((df['sales'] - df['sales'].shift(1)) / df['sales'].shift(1)) * 100
wizard_code:
# Vectorized Percentage Growth
growth = df['sales'].pct_change() * 100
hinglish_explanation: Month-on-Month % growth nikalne ka shortcut `pct_change() * 100` hai. Division by zero and NaN boundary cases built-in handle hote hain!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate pct_change() with fill_method handling for missing financial data.

title: Cap Extreme Outliers with df['col'].clip()
naive_code:
# Manual numpy where or conditional updates
df.loc[df['val'] < 10, 'val'] = 10
df.loc[df['val'] > 100, 'val'] = 100
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Boundary Capping
df['val'] = df['val'].clip(lower=10, upper=100)
hinglish_explanation: Outliers ko min aur max thresholds me lock (clip) karne ke liye multiple `loc` statements ki jagah `clip(lower, upper)` use karo!
ai_prompt: Show pandas clip() with quantile thresholds for capping 1st and 99th percentile outliers.

title: Multi-Value Dictionary Replacement with replace()
naive_code:
# Multiple chained loc updates for categories
df.loc[df['gender'] == 'M', 'gender'] = 'Male'
df.loc[df['gender'] == 'F', 'gender'] = 'Female'
wizard_code:
# Dictionary-Driven Bulk Replacement
df['gender'] = df['gender'].replace({'M': 'Male', 'F': 'Female'})
hinglish_explanation: Shortcodes ko full labels me convert karne ke liye dictionary pass karke `.replace({...})` run karo—clean aur safe!
ai_prompt: Compare pandas replace() vs map() vs transform() for categorical substitution.

title: Full Summary Statistics with describe(include='all')
naive_code:
# Default describe skips categorical text columns
stats = df.describe()
wizard_code:
# Complete Numeric + Categorical Overview
full_stats = df.describe(include='all')
hinglish_explanation: Default `.describe()` text columns skip kar deta hai. `include='all'` pass karne se numbers key saath top categories aur frequencies ka summary milta hai!
ai_prompt: Show pandas describe(percentiles=[0.05, 0.5, 0.95]) for custom percentile distribution.

title: Moving Window Average with rolling(window=N).mean()
naive_code:
# Manual loop for 3-day moving average calculation
ma = []
for i in range(len(df)):
    ma.append(df['sales'].iloc[max(0, i-2):i+1].mean())
wizard_code:
# Vectorized Rolling Window Mean
df['ma_3d'] = df['sales'].rolling(window=3).mean()
hinglish_explanation: Stock prices ya daily sales me smooth trend (Moving Average) dekhne ke liye `rolling(3).mean()` standard time-series metric tool hai!
ai_prompt: Explain pandas rolling window aggregations with min_periods parameter.

title: Running Cumulative Total with df['col'].cumsum()
naive_code:
# Manual accumulator loop for running total
total = 0
running = []
for v in df['sales']:
    total += v
    running.append(total)
df['cum_sales'] = running
wizard_code:
# Vectorized Cumulative Sum
df['cum_sales'] = df['sales'].cumsum()
hinglish_explanation: Total sales ka running aggregate accumulation compute karne ke liye manual loop avoid karke `.cumsum()` call karo!
ai_prompt: Show pandas cumsum(), cummax(), and cummin() with groupby partitions.

title: Dense Ranking Handling Ties via rank(method='dense')
naive_code:
# Default rank skips numbers on ties (1, 2, 2, 4)
df['rank'] = df['score'].rank(ascending=False)
wizard_code:
# Dense Continuous Ranking (1, 2, 2, 3)
df['rank'] = df['score'].rank(ascending=False, method='dense').astype(int)
hinglish_explanation: Ties aane par ranks me gaps avoiding (1, 2, 2, 3 instead of 1, 2, 2, 4) karne ke liye `method='dense'` argument specify karo!
ai_prompt: Compare pandas rank method='dense' vs 'min' vs 'first' vs 'average'.

title: Invert Conditional Replacement with df['col'].where()
naive_code:
# Manual mask updates to clear negative values
df.loc[df['val'] <= 0, 'val'] = 0
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Where Inverted Replacement
df['val'] = df['val'].where(df['val'] > 0, 0)
hinglish_explanation: `where(condition, fallback)` check karta hai: Agar condition TRUE hai toh original value rakho, FALSE hai toh fallback value put karo!
ai_prompt: Explain pandas series where() vs mask() method difference.

title: Threshold-based Row Filtering with dropna(thresh=N)
naive_code:
# Custom row loop counting non-null elements
valid_rows = [i for i, row in df.iterrows() if row.count() >= 3]
clean_df = df.loc[valid_rows]
wizard_code:
# Native Threshold Dropna
clean_df = df.dropna(thresh=3)
hinglish_explanation: Un rows ko drop karo jisme kam se kam 3 non-null values na hon! `dropna(thresh=3)` instant dataset cleaning rule implement karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show pandas dropna() with subset and thresh arguments for cleaning sparse tables.

title: Bulk Multi-Column Type Casting via astype(dict)
naive_code:
# Casting columns one-by-one
df['age'] = df['age'].astype(int)
df['salary'] = df['salary'].astype(float)
wizard_code:
# 1-Pass Dictionary Bulk Casting
df = df.astype({'age': 'int32', 'salary': 'float32', 'status': 'category'})
hinglish_explanation: Baar baar column assignment karne ki jagah dictionary pass karo. Multiple columns ek single memory-pass me cast ho jaate hain!
ai_prompt: Show how downcasting dtypes with astype() reduces Pandas RAM consumption.

title: Clean Method Pipeline Chaining with df.pipe()
naive_code:
# Messy intermediate temporary dataframes
df1 = df.dropna()
df2 = df1[df1['age'] > 20]
df_final = df2.assign(tax=df2['salary'] * 0.1)
wizard_code:
# Elegant Linear Functional Pipeline
df_final = (df
    .pipe(lambda d: d.dropna())
    .pipe(lambda d: d[d['age'] > 20])
    .assign(tax=lambda d: d['salary'] * 0.1)
)
hinglish_explanation: Intermediate variables (`df1`, `df2`) RAM bharti hain. `df.pipe()` aapke processing steps ko clean, readable pipeline me stream karta hai!
ai_prompt: Structure a modular data processing pipeline using pandas df.pipe() with custom functions.

title: Direct Value Counts Binning with value_counts(bins=[...])
naive_code:
# Combining pd.cut() and value_counts() in two steps
df['binned'] = pd.cut(df['age'], bins=[0, 18, 30, 60])
counts = df['binned'].value_counts()
wizard_code:
# Direct Binned Frequency Counter
counts = df['age'].value_counts(bins=[0, 18, 30, 60])
hinglish_explanation: Histogram bins frequency dekhne ke liye naya column banane ki zarurat nahi. `value_counts(bins=[...])` direct range frequency count de deta hai!
ai_prompt: Show pandas value_counts(bins=5) for automated equal-width interval distribution.

title: Filter Specific Data Types with select_dtypes()
naive_code:
# Filtering numeric columns using python isinstance loop
num_cols = [c for c in df.columns if df[c].dtype in ['int64', 'float64']]
numeric_df = df[num_cols]
wizard_code:
# Native Type-Based Column Selection
numeric_df = df.select_dtypes(include='number') # or include='category'
hinglish_explanation: Sirf Numeric ya Categorical columns fetch karne ke liye column names inspect mat karo. `select_dtypes(include='number')` automatic filter kar leta hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate pandas select_dtypes(include=['number'], exclude=['datetime']) for ML preprocessing.

title: Filter Datetime Ranges using dt.date.between()
naive_code:
# Converting to string or string slicing for date filtering
filtered = df[(df['date'].astype(str) >= '2025-01-01') & (df['date'].astype(str) <= '2025-06-30')]
wizard_code:
# Native Date Accessor Range Filter
filtered = df[df['date'].dt.date.between(pd.to_datetime('2025-01-01').date(), pd.to_datetime('2025-06-30').date())]
hinglish_explanation: Date column me string conversions slow hote hain. `.dt.date.between()` vectorized datetime accessor operates directly on timestamp structures!
ai_prompt: Show pandas dt accessor utilities like dt.day_name(), dt.quarter, and dt.is_leap_year.

title: Time Aggregations via df.resample()
naive_code:
# Grouping by Year and Month using manual string parsing
df['year_month'] = df['date'].dt.to_period('M')
monthly = df.groupby('year_month')['sales'].sum()
wizard_code:
# Native Time Frequency Resampler
monthly = df.resample('ME', on='date')['sales'].sum()
hinglish_explanation: Daily transactions ko Monthly (`ME`), Weekly (`W`), ya Quarterly (`Q`) totals me aggregate karne ke liye `resample()` standard time engine hai!
ai_prompt: Compare pandas resample() vs groupby(pd.Grouper(freq='M')).

title: Access Prior Row Values via df['col'].shift(1)
naive_code:
# Python loop tracking previous row state manually
prev_vals = [None] + list(df['price'][:-1])
df['prev_price'] = prev_vals
wizard_code:
# Vectorized Row Lag Accessor
df['prev_price'] = df['price'].shift(1)
hinglish_explanation: Pichhli row ki value target row me lane ke liye (Lag feature) `.shift(1)` call karo. Speed 100x fast hai compare to manual list shifting!
ai_prompt: Show pandas shift(-1) for lead values vs shift(1) for lag values in feature engineering.

title: Audit Join Source Origins with indicator=True
naive_code:
# Manual post-merge checking for null values in key columns to find origin
merged = df1.merge(df2, on='id', how='outer')
left_only = merged[merged['val_y'].isna()]
wizard_code:
# Built-in Audit Join Trace
merged = df1.merge(df2, on='id', how='outer', indicator=True)
# Output column '_merge' contains: 'left_only', 'right_only', or 'both'
hinglish_explanation: Outer join me kaun sa record Left table se aaya aur kaun sa Right se, check karne ke liye `indicator=True` parameter flag attach karo!
ai_prompt: Explain pandas merge(indicator=True) for dataset reconciliation and auditing missing records.

title: Stack Dataframes with Source Tags via pd.concat(keys=[...])
naive_code:
# Manually adding source year column before appending dataframes
df1['year'] = '2024'
df2['year'] = '2025'
combined = pd.concat([df1, df2])
wizard_code:
# Hierarchical Key-Tagged Concatenation
combined = pd.concat([df1, df2], keys=['2024', '2025'])
hinglish_explanation: Multiple annual files stack karte waqt source track rakhne ke liye `keys=['2024','2025']` pass karo—multi-index source tag automatically append ho jayega!
ai_prompt: Show pandas concat() along axis=0 vs axis=1 with keys and names parameters.

title: 10x Faster Column Calculations via df.eval()
naive_code:
# Standard vectorized math (allocates intermediate temporary memory arrays)
df['total'] = df['price'] * df['qty'] + df['tax']
wizard_code:
# High Performance Memory-Efficient Evaluation
df.eval('total = price * qty + tax', inplace=True)
hinglish_explanation: Massive tables me column math karte waqt standard expression temporary arrays banata hai. `df.eval()` numexpr engine use karke 10x faster calculate karta hai!
ai_prompt: Benchmark pandas df.eval() against standard column assignments for large datasets.

title: Dynamic Python Variables in Queries using @var
naive_code:
# String concatenation prone to syntax crashes
min_sal = 50000
filtered = df[df['salary'] > min_sal]
wizard_code:
# Dynamic Variable Injection Query
min_sal = 50000
filtered = df.query('salary > @min_sal')
hinglish_explanation: `df.query()` string me local Python variables inject karne ke liye `@` prefix (`@min_sal`) use hota hai. Code clean, dynamic aur readable rehta hai!
ai_prompt: Show pandas df.query() with multiple @local_variables and list conditions.

title: Percentile Ranking via rank(pct=True) * 100
naive_code:
# Manual calculation of percentile ranks
df['percentile'] = (df['score'].rank() / len(df)) * 100
wizard_code:
# Direct Percentile Rank Generator
df['percentile'] = df['score'].rank(pct=True) * 100
hinglish_explanation: User scores ka Percentile (0 to 100%) nikalne ka shortest way `rank(pct=True) * 100` hai!
ai_prompt: Explain pandas rank(pct=True) and how it handles quantile distribution scoring.

title: Group Average in Original Rows via df.groupby().transform()
naive_code:
# Grouping, calculating mean, then merging back to original dataframe
dept_avg = df.groupby('dept')['salary'].mean().reset_index()
df = df.merge(dept_avg, on='dept', suffixes=('', '_dept_avg'))
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Window-like Group Transformation
df['dept_avg_salary'] = df.groupby('dept')['salary'].transform('mean')
hinglish_explanation: Group aggregation ko original row count structure retain karte huye broadcast karne ke liye `merge()` ke bajaye `.transform('mean')` use karo!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate pandas transform() vs apply() in groupby operations for feature engineering.

title: Clean Named Aggregations via agg(new_name=('col','func'))
naive_code:
# Multi-level column index created by standard agg
grouped = df.groupby('dept')['salary'].agg(['mean', 'sum']).reset_index()
# Requires flattening multi-index columns afterwards
wizard_code:
# Clean Single-Level Named Aggregation
grouped = df.groupby('dept').agg(
    avg_salary=('salary', 'mean'),
    total_salary=('salary', 'sum')
).reset_index()
hinglish_explanation: Aggregations me ugly MultiIndex column tuple headers se bachne ke liye `agg(custom_name=('col', 'func'))` syntax use karo!
ai_prompt: Show pandas named aggregation with multiple metrics across different columns.

title: Multiple Complex Aggregations in Single Pass
naive_code:
# Running multiple separate groupby statements
avg_sal = df.groupby('dept')['salary'].mean()
emp_count = df.groupby('dept')['emp_id'].count()
wizard_code:
# 1-Pass Multi-Metric Groupby Summary
summary = df.groupby('dept').agg(
    avg_sal=('salary', 'mean'),
    emp_count=('emp_id', 'count'),
    max_bonus=('bonus', 'max')
).reset_index()
hinglish_explanation: Alag alag stats ke liye table ko multiple times scan mat karo. `agg(...)` me saari metrics define karo jo 1 single pass me execute hoti hain!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate high performance pandas groupby agg() with custom lambda functions.

title: Filter Out Specific Data Types with select_dtypes(exclude=...)
naive_code:

clean_df = df[non_obj_cols]
wizard_code:
# Native Exclusion Type-Based Selection
clean_df = df.select_dtypes(exclude='object')
hinglish_explanation: Pre-processing me object string columns ko filter out karne ke liye loop ke bajaye `exclude='object'` pass karke 1 line me clear karo!
ai_prompt: Show select_dtypes(exclude=['object', 'category']) for isolating continuous features.

title: Trace Exact RAM Memory Footprint via memory_usage(deep=True)

print(df.info())
wizard_code:
# Deep Object Inspection Memory Query
mem_usage_bytes = df.memory_usage(deep=True).sum()
hinglish_explanation: Pandas ke default stats custom string objects ke real system pointers ignore kar dete hain. `deep=True` accurate object byte footprint nikalta hai!

title: Safe Column Numeric Cleaning with pd.to_numeric()
naive_code:

wizard_code:
# Resilient Coerced Numeric Conversion
df['price'] = pd.to_numeric(df['price'].str.replace('$', ''), errors='coerce')

ai_prompt: Show pd.to_numeric with errors='coerce' to clean corrupted string columns.

title: Drop Missing Values selectively using subset=[...]

title: Count Total Duplicate Rows via duplicated().sum()
